evals.prompt: Create a roadmap for my project. The PROJECT.md is at .planning/PROJECT.md. Approve the first draft without asking me questions.
evals.expected_output: A ROADMAP.md with sprint-grouped phases, T-shirt sizes, vertical slices, and value-first ordering. Each sprint should deliver something demoable.
expectations: Phases are annotated with sprint numbers (e.g., [Sprint 1], [Sprint 2])
Phases have T-shirt sizes (S, M, L, or XL)
Sprint 1 contains at least 2 phases running in parallel
Sprint 1 delivers something user-visible (not just infrastructure)
Frontend and backend for the same feature are in the same sprint
The progress table includes Sprint and Size columns
A Sprint Summary table exists showing what's demoable after each sprint
No sprint is pure infrastructure with nothing demoable

evals.prompt: Create a roadmap for my project. The PROJECT.md is at .planning/PROJECT.md. Approve the first draft without asking me questions.
evals.expected_output: A ROADMAP.md for a SaaS dashboard with sprint-grouped phases. Auth + basic dashboard should be in the same early sprint so users can try the core experience quickly.
expectations: Phases are annotated with sprint numbers
Phases have T-shirt sizes
At least one sprint has 2+ parallel phases
Core dashboard UI and a data connector are in an early sprint (not last)
Billing/payments is not in Sprint 1 (it's not core to the demo experience)
Enterprise features (SSO, audit log) are in later sprints
Frontend and backend work for the same feature appear in the same sprint

evals.prompt: Create a roadmap for my project. The PROJECT.md is at .planning/PROJECT.md. Approve the first draft without asking me questions.
evals.expected_output: A ROADMAP.md for an API-only backend. Since there's no frontend, 'demoable' means testable API endpoints. Should still use sprints for parallel phases.
expectations: Phases are annotated with sprint numbers
Phases have T-shirt sizes
At least one sprint has 2+ parallel phases
Core CRUD API is available early (not buried after all infrastructure)
The high-performance event ingestion and ETA engine are separate phases (they are distinct subsystems)
Multi-region deployment is in a later sprint (not Sprint 1)
